summary: Independent QA accepts M7-CORE-ACTION-LOOP-UX-001 after reviewing implementation commit e346153264fa96786613771c865a3576e828d712, R2 implementation commit e55b039fa3db907b875bbee0dc2b6097749e07f1, the writer/gameplay handoffs, scope boundaries, and focused implementation evidence. The phase/action loop, obligation entry path, District 1 obligation discoverability, appointment preview/confirmation feedback, duplicate-submit guard, and command/read-model authority boundary satisfy the task acceptance criteria. The gameplay REQUEST_CHANGES items are closed in R2: player-mode obligation handling no longer exposes read-model/read-only/command-path/GameCommand wording, and post-click feedback is tied to District 1 troop obligation support.

tests_run.result: PASS before QA handoff write; current branch was codex/m7-core-action-loop-ux with no working-tree changes.
PASS scope check; R1 implementation touched only apps/web styling, packages/ui UI/i18n, and focused UI/E2E tests.
PASS scope check; R2 implementation touched only apps/web styling, packages/ui UI/i18n, and focused UI/E2E tests.
PASS for reviewed implementation range; no forbidden sim-core, protocol, save-format, content-source, Electron, desktop, server, telemetry, final-art, or manual-battle paths were present.
PASS; no whitespace errors in the reviewed implementation range.

PASS; no whitespace errors through current review HEAD before QA handoff.
PASS; writer R1 handoff is VALID.
PASS; gameplay R1 REQUEST_CHANGES handoff is VALID.
PASS; writer R2 handoff is VALID.
PASS; gameplay R2 ACCEPT handoff is VALID.

PASS review inspection; relevant implementation paths and tests are present. R2 obligation panel/result strings use player-facing support wording. Remaining read-model mentions are preexisting map/debug/test/workspace context or negative test assertions, not the repaired obligation player flow.

decisions: ACCEPT: The first-screen core action loop exposes current phase, main unresolved decision, and next primary action, with the District 1 obligation prioritized by default unless the player intentionally enters appointment flow.
ACCEPT: Task rail, selected district context, and district inspector paths route to the same obligation handling surface and focus the relevant district.
ACCEPT: Appointment preview shows before/after, cost, benefit, risk, and will-change information; confirmation moves to result state with submitted/accepted/rejected or pending semantics.
ACCEPT: Duplicate appointment confirmation is guarded by submitted appointment key and disabled/relabelled accepted/submitted states.

ACCEPT: UI changes preserve existing command/query/read-model boundaries. The obligation support affordance calls the existing M4 command submission path and does not mutate authoritative WorldState directly.
ACCEPT: R2 closes gameplay REQUEST_CHANGES by removing player-mode obligation engineering wording and adding District 1 troop obligation support feedback after click.

ACCEPT: The R2 writer's root pnpm test timeout is acceptable evidence-wise because the failed command hit unrelated long-running sim tests at the default Vitest timeout, while direct focused Vitest, Playwright web-shell coverage, pnpm check, build:web, storybook:build, diff checks, and handoff/task validation passed. No lead rerun is required for this QA decision.

acceptance_criteria.result: Satisfied by PlayerFirstScreenBrief/core action-loop i18n and Playwright/static assertions for Current phase, Main unresolved decision, Next primary action, and Obligation phase.
Satisfied by handleOpenObligations, task rail obligation card/drawer, selected district context, and DistrictPanel onReviewObligations using the same obligation drawer.
Satisfied by focused District 1 obligation panel, selected context Handle obligation action, and tests asserting District 1 plus Prepare obligation support.
Satisfied by AppointmentImpactPreview and Playwright assertions for Before, After, Cost, Benefit, and Risk.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: post-confirm submitted/accepted/rejected/pending feedback and duplicate guard
acceptance_criteria.result: Satisfied by deriveM3AppointmentSubmissionState, formatAppointmentConfirmAction, AppointmentResultFeedback, data-submission-state assertions, disabled Accepted button, and status role feedback.
Satisfied by code inspection showing UI submits existing created command DTOs via onM3CommandSubmit/onM4CommandSubmit and only updates local UI state for drawer/flow/selection.

evidence.scope_boundary: Reviewed implementation commits and current branch process commits stay within allowed Web/UI/tests/project message/task paths. No sim-core, sim-ai, protocol, save-format, platform, Electron, desktop, content-source, tools, docs gate/roadmap, telemetry, server, final art, or manual node battle scope expansion was found.
evidence.route_evidence: project/messages/routes/M7-CORE-ACTION-LOOP-UX-001__ROUTE-20260630-GAMEPLAY-R2-TO-QA-REVIEW.json exists and records qa_reviewer route to thread 019f1838-adf5-7020-a50d-c21adc594d70 for review commit e55b039fa3db907b875bbee0dc2b6097749e07f1.

risks.summary: Writer R2 recorded one failed root pnpm test attempt caused by unrelated long-running sim tests timing out at 5000 ms before the targeted UI file. Focused UI Vitest, Playwright, pnpm check, build:web, storybook:build, and diff checks passed, so this is not a blocking QA risk and does not require lead rerun.

The existing generic Campaign request prepared status may remain visible because the task correctly avoided protocol/sim changes. R2 adds adjacent District 1 troop obligation support feedback, which is sufficient for this low-fidelity UX recovery.
Broader map/decision-surface synchronization remains intentionally out of scope for M7-MAP-DECISION-SURFACE-SYNC-001.

requested_action: Lead may close M7-CORE-ACTION-LOOP-UX-001 as accepted and continue the systemic interaction recovery chain. Do not treat this as formal M7 content-lock approval.
